standard_position: PortSwigger requires English law as governing law and the exclusive jurisdiction of the English courts.
suggested_redline: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales. Each party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales.

standard_position: PortSwigger accepts confidentiality obligations of up to 5 years from disclosure. Perpetual obligations or terms exceeding 5 years require escalation.
suggested_redline: The confidentiality obligations in this Agreement shall survive for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure of the relevant Confidential Information.

issue: Short or missing confidentiality term (under 2 years)
standard_position: PortSwigger's standard confidentiality term is 3 years from disclosure. Anything under 2 years should be flagged.
suggested_redline: The confidentiality obligations shall survive termination or expiry of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years.

issue: Overly broad definition of confidential information with no standard carve-outs
standard_position: Definitions of Confidential Information must include standard carve-outs: publicly available information, independently developed information, information received from third parties without restriction, and information required to be disclosed by law.
suggested_redline: Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available other than through breach of this Agreement; (b) was already known to the receiving party at time of disclosure; (c) is independently developed without use of the Confidential Information; or (d) is required to be disclosed by law, regulation or court order.

issue: Uncapped liability or liability cap set below total fees paid
standard_position: PortSwigger's aggregate liability shall not exceed the total fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim. Consequential loss exclusions are standard. Neither party should accept unlimited liability.
suggested_redline: Each party's total aggregate liability arising under or in connection with this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid or payable by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, special, incidental, or consequential loss or damage.

issue: Clause assigning PortSwigger IP ownership to the other party
standard_position: PortSwigger retains ownership of all its pre-existing IP and any IP developed during the engagement. Any attempt to transfer ownership of PortSwigger's background IP is a red line and requires immediate escalation.
suggested_redline: Each party retains all ownership rights in its Background IP. No rights in either party's Background IP are transferred under this Agreement except as expressly stated herein.

standard_position: PortSwigger's standard payment terms are 30 days net from invoice date. Terms exceeding 30 days require finance approval.
suggested_redline: Payment is due within 30 days of invoice date. Late payments shall accrue interest at 8% above the Bank of England base rate.

issue: Auto-renewal clause with insufficient cancellation notice period
standard_position: Auto-renewal clauses are acceptable provided the notice period for cancellation is at least 30 days. Less than 30 days notice period should be flagged.
suggested_redline: This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one-year periods unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least 30 days prior to the end of the then-current term.

issue: Missing or inadequate UK GDPR and data protection provisions
standard_position: Any contract involving personal data must reference UK GDPR compliance. A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) may be required. Flag any contract involving personal data that lacks explicit data protection provisions.
suggested_redline: Each party shall comply with its obligations under applicable data protection legislation including the UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018. Where either party processes personal data on behalf of the other, a separate Data Processing Agreement shall be entered into prior to such processing.

issue: Non-solicitation clause exceeding 12 months or covering blanket hiring restrictions
standard_position: PortSwigger accepts non-solicitation of specific named individuals for up to 12 months. Blanket hiring restrictions (i.e. all employees) or terms exceeding 12 months should be pushed back.
suggested_redline: For a period of 12 months following termination of this Agreement, neither party shall solicit for employment any employee of the other party who was directly involved in the performance of this Agreement, without prior written consent.

issue: Confidentiality clause does not permit disclosure to professional advisors
standard_position: PortSwigger requires that confidentiality clauses permit disclosure to legal, financial, and other professional advisors under an equivalent duty of confidence.
suggested_redline: Confidential Information may be disclosed to professional advisers (legal, financial, or otherwise) of the receiving party who are bound by professional duties of confidence or equivalent written obligations.

issue: Termination for convenience with less than 30 days notice or immediate termination without cause
standard_position: Termination for convenience requires at least 30 days written notice. Immediate termination is acceptable only for material breach (with cure period) or insolvency.
suggested_redline: Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon 30 days written notice to the other party. Either party may terminate immediately upon written notice if the other party is in material breach and (where the breach is remediable) has failed to remedy such breach within 14 days of notice.

issue: One-sided indemnification in favour of the counterparty only
standard_position: Indemnification should be mutual. One-sided indemnities that only run in favour of the counterparty require escalation. IP infringement indemnities are acceptable if mutual and capped.
suggested_redline: Each party (the Indemnifying Party) shall indemnify and hold harmless the other party from and against any losses, damages, and costs arising from the Indemnifying Party's breach of this Agreement or infringement of a third party's intellectual property rights.

issue: Unrestricted assignment rights granted to the counterparty without consent
standard_position: Assignment requires prior written consent from both parties. Permitted exceptions (no consent needed): assignment to affiliates and assignment in connection with a change of control (with prior notice only).
suggested_redline: Neither party may assign its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, except to an affiliate or in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets (in which case prior written notice shall suffice).

issue: Missing entire agreement and non-reliance clause
standard_position: All commercial agreements should contain an entire agreement clause superseding prior negotiations and representations. Flag its absence.
suggested_redline: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior agreements, representations, warranties and understandings of the parties with respect thereto.

issue: Force majeure clause covering events within a party's reasonable control
standard_position: Force majeure clauses are acceptable provided they do not cover events within the party's reasonable control. Financial difficulty and supply chain issues should not be accepted as force majeure events.
suggested_redline: Neither party shall be in breach of this Agreement nor liable for delay or failure to perform any obligation if such delay or failure results from events, circumstances or causes beyond its reasonable control, excluding financial difficulty or inability to pay.
